Padded.special_description: Light armor made from quilted layers of cloth and batting.

Leather.special_description: Light armor made from hardened leather. Flexible and quiet.

Studded Leather.special_description: Light armor. Hardened leather reinforced with metal studs or small plates.

Chain Shirt.special_description: Light armor. A shirt of chain links, typically worn under normal clothing. Comes with a steel cap.

Hide.special_description: Medium armor. Thick, stiff hides and furs. Noisy but cheap.

Scale Mail.special_description: Medium armor. Metal scales sewn or riveted to a leather backing.

Chainmail.special_description: Medium armor. Standard chain links with padded underlayer. The suit includes gauntlets.

Breastplate.special_description: Medium armor. A fitted breastplate of steel. Comes with a helmet and greaves. Good protection-to-mobility ratio.

Splint Mail.special_description: Heavy armor. Narrow vertical strips of metal riveted to a leather or cloth backing. When running in heavy armor, move only triple speed.

Banded Mail.special_description: Heavy armor. Horizontal strips (bands) of metal over chain or leather. Includes gauntlets. When running in heavy armor, move only triple speed.

Half-Plate.special_description: Heavy armor. Partial plate over a chain shirt or similar, covering vital areas. Excellent protection. When running in heavy armor, move only triple speed.

Full Plate.special_description: Heavy armor. Complete suit of interlocking plates. Includes gauntlets, heavy leather boots, visored helmet, and thick padding. Must be individually fitted (resizing captured suit: 200-800 gp). When running in heavy armor, move only triple speed.

Buckler.special_description: Small metal shield strapped to the forearm. Allows bow/crossbow use without penalty. Can wield weapon in shield arm at -1 attack penalty (stacks). No AC bonus the rest of the round after attacking with off-hand weapon. Cannot be used to bash.

Shield, light wooden.special_description: Light wooden shield. Can be used for shield bash attacks. Hand not free to cast spells while bashing.

Shield, light steel.special_description: Light steel shield. More durable than wooden. Can be used for shield bash attacks.

Shield, heavy wooden.special_description: Heavy wooden shield. Good protection. Can be used for shield bash attacks.

Shield, heavy steel.special_description: Heavy steel shield. Standard heavy shield. Can be used for shield bash attacks. Hand not free to cast spells.

Tower Shield.special_description: Massive shield. Can instead grant cover (often superior to the +4 shield bonus, especially against ranged). High arcane failure and check penalty. Hand not free.

Armor Spikes.special_description: Spikes added to any suit of armor. Deal extra piercing damage on successful grapple (use martial weapon damage from weapons table). Count as martial weapon for proficiency. -4 on grapple checks if not proficient. Can also be used for regular melee or off-hand light weapon attacks. Not improved by armor enhancement bonus (but can be enchanted separately as a weapon). Cannot attack with spikes if you already attacked with another off-hand weapon (and vice versa).

Gauntlet, locked.special_description: Armored gauntlet with chains/braces to lock a held weapon to the hand (+10 vs. disarm attempts). Attaching or removing a weapon: full-round action provoking AoO. Weight only if used with light/no armor or breastplate (otherwise replaces existing gauntlet). While locked the hand cannot be used for somatic spell components or most skills. Allows lethal unarmed damage like a normal gauntlet.

Shield Spikes.special_description: Spikes added to a shield, turning shield bashes into more damaging martial weapon attacks (use spiked shield damage from weapons table). Spikes can be enchanted separately. Do not increase the shield's AC bonus.
